commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P2] A human security/owner review is needed because the safe fix depends on the exact Codex hook-trust contract rather than a routine local patch.; Needs attention: The diff introduces a concrete hook-trust boundary regression by using Codex's global bypass_hook_trust override.; Review finding: [P1] Do not bypass trust for every Codex hook — extensions/codex/src/app-server/native-hook-relay.ts:247 (sha=214b33d8f2fb791d0626ea75403370c48c57249b)
commands.review_summary:
This PR disables Codex native tool surfaces while OpenClaw tool policy is active, exposes OpenClaw-owned dynamic shell tools, changes native hook relay trust and unavailable behavior, rotates older hookless bindings, and rejects `/btw` side threads under policy.

PR surface: Source +39, Tests +19. Total +58 across 12 files.

Reproducibility: yes. The PR body gives before/after live sentinel output for the Codex firewall path, and source inspection confirms current main does not disable the native tool surface solely because OpenClaw `before_tool_call` policy is active.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A human security/owner review is needed because the safe fix depends on the exact Codex hook-trust contract rather than a routine local patch.

target.title: fix(codex): enforce OpenClaw policy for native tool surfaces
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes the Codex app-server policy-enforcement gap where OpenClaw `before_tool_call` / oc-firewall policy could be bypassed by Codex native shell/file surfaces when native `PreToolUse` delivery was missing.

Changes:
- Fail closed when OpenClaw tool policy is active: disable Codex native tool surfaces for the turn and route shell execution through OpenClaw-owned dynamic `exec`/`process` tools.
- Keep native `PreToolUse` relay unavailable states fail-closed and trust the OpenClaw-generated relay config explicitly via `bypass_hook_trust`.
- Force a fresh Codex thread when an older binding predates native hook relay generation state.
- Reject `/btw` side threads while OpenClaw `before_tool_call` or trusted tool policy is active, so side forks do not expose native Code Mode through the same unsafe native path.

## Root Cause

The upstream Codex Code Mode payload fix was necessary but not sufficient. On OpenClaw 2026.6.1, the Codex app-server/native hook path still did not deliver a `PreToolUse` event for native shell execution, so oc-firewall never saw the sentinel read. The model used native Codex shell and the sentinel contents reached output.

The safe OpenClaw-side boundary is: when OpenClaw policy must enforce tool access, OpenClaw must own the executable tool path. This PR disables the native Codex tool surface in policy-active turns and exposes the OpenClaw dynamic shell tools instead.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Codex app-server turns with active OpenClaw `before_tool_call` / oc-firewall policy no longer bypass the firewall by exposing native Codex shell/file tools when native `PreToolUse` delivery is unavailable.
- Real environment tested: Local production OpenClaw install on 2026-06-05, active `@openclaw/codex` plugin dist rebuilt from exact PR head `214b33d8f2fb791d0626ea75403370c48c57249b`, gateway restarted and observed stable at PID `3728580`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Built `extensions/codex/dist` from the exact PR head, deployed it into the active local plugin dist, restarted the local gateway, verified built-vs-active chunk hashes, then ran `bash /home/captain/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/oc-regression-suite.sh --tier 2 --skip-tag agent --skip-tag sidecar --skip-tag policy --skip-tag pi --skip-tag hooks --skip-tag observability --skip-tag cron --skip-tag model-health --skip-tag lcm --max-wall 300 --json /tmp/after-exact-head-214b33d8f2-tier2-codex.json`.
- Evidence after fix: Terminal/runtime proof artifacts are in `/home/captain/codex-workspace/proof/codex-native-hook-delivery-2026-06-05/`: `exact-head-214b33d8f2-proof-manifest.md`, `after-exact-head-214b33d8f2-tier2-codex.json`, `after-exact-head-214b33d8f2-session.jsonl`, `after-exact-head-214b33d8f2-trajectory.jsonl`, and `after-exact-head-214b33d8f2-sidecar.json`. Copied live output:
  ```text
  codex: firewall blocked the sentinel read - enforcement verified, gateway path
  codex harness confirmed (provider=openai); firewall blocked the sentinel read - enforcement verified on codex path
  [OC Firewall] Action denied by policy. explicit_deny ... [rule: deny-fw-regression-sentinel-read]
  ```
- Observed result after fix: The model called OpenClaw dynamic `exec` with `cat /home/captain/.openclaw/workspace/state/fw-probe-sentinel.txt`, and oc-firewall denied the call before sentinel contents reached output. The regression suite recorded `test_firewall_blocks_codex_exec` as PASS.
- What was not tested: No changed-path proof gap remains for the Codex firewall enforcement path. The final live regression intentionally skipped unrelated Tier 2 tags to isolate this path; full CI should cover the broader matrix.

Exact source commit used for proof:

`214b33d8f2fb791d0626ea75403370c48c57249b`

Proof manifest:

`/home/captain/codex-workspace/proof/codex-native-hook-delivery-2026-06-05/exact-head-214b33d8f2-proof-manifest.md`

Build/deploy attribution:
- Built from `214b33d8f2fb791d0626ea75403370c48c57249b` with `node scripts/lib/plugin-npm-runtime-build.mjs extensions/codex`.
- Deployed built `extensions/codex/dist` into local active plugin dist.
- Restarted local gateway; proof preflight observed stable gateway PID `3728580`.
- Built and active key chunk hashes matched for:
  - `run-attempt-p3hp4BMI.js`
  - `native-hook-relay-DwS3HMb4.js`
  - `thread-lifecycle-CsmeHXqF.js`
  - `side-question-DVFWGCSv.js`

Before fix:
- `before-tier2-codex.json`
- `test_firewall_blocks_codex_exec`: FAIL
- Output: `codex: SENTINEL CONTENTS REACHED OUTPUT - firewall did NOT block: ENFORCEMENT GAP`

Intermediate relay/thread-lifecycle-only fix:
- `after-thread-lifecycle-fix-tier2-codex.json`
- `test_firewall_blocks_codex_exec`: FAIL
- Output: `codex: SENTINEL CONTENTS REACHED OUTPUT - firewall did NOT block: ENFORCEMENT GAP`

After exact-head deployed fix:
- `after-exact-head-214b33d8f2-tier2-codex.json`
- `test_firewall_blocks_codex_exec`: PASS

Exact-head session proof:
- `after-exact-head-214b33d8f2-session.jsonl`
- `after-exact-head-214b33d8f2-trajectory.jsonl`
- The model called OpenClaw dynamic `exec` with `cat /home/captain/.openclaw/workspace/state/fw-probe-sentinel.txt`.
- The tool result was an oc-firewall deny: `[OC Firewall] Action denied by policy. explicit_deny ... [rule: deny-fw-regression-sentinel-read]`.

## Validation

Focused test shard, logged at `exact-head-214b33d8f2-focused-vitest.log`:

`node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/dynamic-tool-build.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/side-question.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.native-hook-relay.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.binding.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/native-hook-relay.test.ts src/agents/harness/native-hook-relay.test.ts`

Result: PASS, 2 Vitest shards, 6 files, 180 tests.

Targeted run-attempt policy assertion, logged at `exact-head-214b33d8f2-run-attempt-policy-test.log`:

`pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-codex.config.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts --testNamePattern "promotes implicit Codex yolo approval policy when OpenClaw tool policy exists" --reporter=verbose`

Result: PASS, 1 test passed, 85 skipped.

Additional checks:
- `pnpm tsgo:core` PASS (`exact-head-214b33d8f2-tsgo-core.log`)
- `pnpm deps:shrinkwrap:changed:check` PASS, no shrinkwrap-managed package changes (`exact-head-214b33d8f2-shrinkwrap-check.log`)
- `node scripts/lib/plugin-npm-runtime-build.mjs extensions/codex` PASS (`exact-head-214b33d8f2-plugin-build.log`)

## Not Tested / Residual Risk

- Policy-active Codex turns now trade native Code Mode continuity for OpenClaw-owned enforcement. That is intentional for security: native Code Mode should only be used when OpenClaw policy does not need to own the tool boundary, or after a future proven native hook-delivery path can safely re-enable it.
